name: eclcli

metadata.name: eclcli
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Enterprise Cloud CLI

Enterprise Cloud CLI (a.k.a eclcli) is an [OpenStackClient](https://github.com/openstack/python-openstackclient) based command-line client for NTT Communications' Enterprise Cloud 2.0 that brings the command set for Baremetal, Compute, SSS, Image, Network, Block Storage and various other APIs together in a single shell with a uniform command structure.

The primary goal is to provide a unified user experience for various services provide in ECL2.0 through a uniform command structure.

## Getting Started

Enterprise Cloud CLI can be installed from PyPI using pip.

```bash
$ pip install eclcli
```

Yet it is strongly advised to use [virtualenv](https://virtualenv.pypa.io/en/stable/) to avoid conflicts with system packages, in short:

```bash
$ virtualenv .venv
$ source .venv/bin/activate
$ pip install --upgrade eclcli
```

For experienced users we suggest using [pyenv](https://github.com/pyenv/pyenv) with pyenv-virtualenv plugin:

## Configuration

Before you are able to use CLI you must configure it.

The CLI is configured via command-line, environment variables and `~/.config/ecl/clouds.yaml` file.

Authentication using username/password is most commonly used.

```bash
$ export OS_USERNAME=<username>
$ export OS_PASSWORD=<password>
$ export OS_TENANT_ID=<tenant_id>
$ export OS_AUTH_URL=<auth_url>
$ export OS_PROJECT_DOMAIN_ID=default
$ export OS_USER_DOMAIN_ID=default
```

In certain cases it may be more convenient to use file based configuration using file `~/.config/ecl/clouds.yaml`.

For more information see [ECL tutorial page](https://ecl.ntt.com/en/documents/tutorials/eclc/rsts/installation.html).
Notice, though, that you may need to use `--os-cloud` option parameter to specify which credentials to use from the file.

## Listing command references

There are a few variants on getting help.
A list of global options are supported with `--help`.

```bash
$ ecl --help
```

There is also a `help` command that can be used to get help text for a specific command.

```bash
$ ecl help baremetal server create
```

## Usage

```bash
$ ecl command list
# Returns all available commands

$ ecl baremetal server list
# Returns list of baremetal servers

$ ecl help baremetal
# Returns help for any command
```

## Documentation

Please find more usage documentation on [official site](https://ecl.ntt.com/en/).

## Support

ECL2.0 users can raise requests via NTT Communications' ticket portal.

## Contributing

Please contribute using [Github Flow](https://guides.github.com/introduction/flow/) Create a branch, add commits, and [open a pull request](https://github.com/nttcom/eclcli/compare/).

## License

- Apache 2.0

methods.name: eclcli.common.clientmanager.ClientManager.__init__
documentation:
Set up a ClientManager

```
param cli_options

Options collected from the command-line, environment, or wherever

param api_version

Dict of API versions: key is API name, value is the version

param verify

TLS certificate verification; may be a boolean to enable or disable
server certificate verification, or a filename of a CA certificate
bundle to be used in verification (implies True)

param pw_func

Callback function for asking the user for a password.  The function
takes an optional string for the prompt ('Password: ' on None) and
returns a string containing the password
```

methods.name: eclcli.common.clientmanager.ClientManager.setup_auth
documentation:
Set up authentication

```
param required_scope

indicate whether a scoped token is required
```

This is deferred until authentication is actually attempted because
it gets in the way of things that do not require auth.

```

methods.name: eclcli.common.parseractions.MultiKeyValueAction.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the action object, and parse customized options

Required keys and optional keys can be specified when initializing
the action to enable the key validation. If none of them specified,
the key validation will be skipped.

```
param required_keys

a list of required keys

param optional_keys

a list of optional keys
```

A custom action to parse arguments as key1=value1,key2=value2 pairs

Ensure that `dest` is a list. The list will finally contain multiple
dicts, with key=value pairs in them.

NOTE: The arguments string should be a comma separated key-value pairs.
And comma(',') and equal('=') may not be used in the key or value.

classes.name: eclcli.common.parseractions.RangeAction
documentation:
A custom action to parse a single value or a range of values

Parses single integer values or a range of integer values delimited
by a colon and returns a tuple of integers:
'4' sets `dest` to (4, 4)
'6:9' sets `dest` to (6, 9)

classes.name: eclcli.common.parseractions.NonNegativeAction
documentation:
A custom action to check whether the value is non-negative or not

Ensures the value is >= 0.

Base SSS Exception.

To correctly use this class, inherit from it and define
a 'message' property. That message will get printf'd
with the keyword arguments provided to the constructor.

classes.name: eclcli.sss.sssclient.common.exceptions.SSSClientException
documentation:
Base exception which exceptions from SSS are mapped into.

NOTE: on the client side, we use different exception types in order
to allow client library users to handle server exceptions in try...except
blocks. The actual error message is the one generated on the server side.

e.g., NetworkNotFound -> NetworkNotFoundClient

methods.name: eclcli.sss.sssclient.v2.client.ClientBase.retry_request
documentation:
Call do_request with the default retry configuration.

Only idempotent requests should retry failed connection attempts.
:raises: ConnectionFailed if the maximum # of retries is exceeded

Client for the OpenStack SSS v2 API.

```
param string username

Username for authentication. (optional)

param string user_id

User ID for authentication. (optional)

param string password

Password for authentication. (optional)

param string token

Token for authentication. (optional)

param string tenant_name

Tenant name. (optional)

param string tenant_id

Tenant id. (optional)

param string auth_strategy

'keystone' by default, 'noauth' for no
authentication against keystone. (optional)

param string auth_url

Keystone service endpoint for authorization.

param string service_type

Network service type to pull from the
keystone catalog (e.g. 'network') (optional)

param string endpoint_type

Network service endpoint type to pull from the
keystone catalog (e.g. 'publicURL',
'internalURL', or 'adminURL') (optional)

param string region_name

Name of a region to select when choosing an
endpoint from the service catalog.

param string endpoint_url

A user-supplied endpoint URL for the neutron
service.  Lazy-authentication is possible for API
service calls if endpoint is set at
instantiation.(optional)

param integer timeout

Allows customization of the timeout for client
http requests. (optional)

param bool insecure

SSL certificate validation. (optional)

param bool log_credentials

Allow for logging of passwords or not.
Defaults to False. (optional)

param string ca_cert

SSL CA bundle file to use. (optional)

param integer retries

How many times idempotent (GET, PUT, DELETE)
requests to SSS server should be retried if
they fail (default: 0).

param bool raise_errors

If True then exceptions caused by connection
failure are propagated to the caller.
(default: True)

param session

Keystone client auth session to use. (optional)

param auth

Keystone auth plugin to use. (optional)
```

Example:

```
from sssclient.v2 import client
sss = client.Client(username=USER,
                        password=PASS,
                        tenant_name=TENANT_NAME,
                        auth_url=KEYSTONE_URL)

nets = sss.list_networks()
...
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.discover_auth_systems
documentation:
Discover the available auth-systems.

This won't take into account the old style auth-systems.

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.load_auth_system_opts
documentation:
Load options needed by the available auth-systems into a parser.

This function will try to populate the parser with options from the
available plugins.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.get_auth_url
documentation: Return the auth url for the plugin (if any).

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.add_opts
documentation:
Populate and return the parser with the options for this plugin.

If the plugin does not need any options, it should return the same
parser untouched.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.parse_opts
documentation:
Parse the actual auth-system options if any.

This method is expected to populate the attribute self.opts with a
dict containing the options and values needed to make authentication.
If the dict is empty, the client should assume that it needs the same
options as the 'keystone' auth system (i.e. os_username and
os_password).

Returns the self.opts dict.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.authenticate
documentation: Authenticate using plugin defined method.

Base class for authentication plugins.

An authentication plugin needs to override at least the authenticate
method to be a valid plugin.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.auth_plugin.DeprecatedAuthPlugin
documentation:
Class to mimic the AuthPlugin class for deprecated auth systems.

Old auth systems only define two entry points: openstack.client.auth_url
and openstack.client.authenticate. This class will load those entry points
into a class similar to a valid AuthPlugin.

documentation:

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpError
documentation: The base exception class for all HTTP exceptions.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPClientError
documentation:
Client-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the client seems to have erred.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpServerError
documentation:
Server-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the server is aware that it has
erred or is incapable of performing the request.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadRequest
documentation:
HTTP 400 - Bad Request.

The request cannot be fulfilled due to bad syntax.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Unauthorized
documentation:
HTTP 401 - Unauthorized.

Similar to 403 Forbidden, but specifically for use when authentication
is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PaymentRequired
documentation:
HTTP 402 - Payment Required.

Reserved for future use.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Forbidden
documentation:
HTTP 403 - Forbidden.

The request was a valid request, but the server is refusing to respond
to it.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotFound
documentation:
HTTP 404 - Not Found.

The requested resource could not be found but may be available again
in the future.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.MethodNotAllowed
documentation:
HTTP 405 - Method Not Allowed.

A request was made of a resource using a request method not supported
by that resource.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotAcceptable
documentation:
HTTP 406 - Not Acceptable.

The requested resource is only capable of generating content not
acceptable according to the Accept headers sent in the request.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ProxyAuthenticationRequired
documentation:
HTTP 407 - Proxy Authentication Required.

The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 408 - Request Timeout.

The server timed out waiting for the request.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Conflict
documentation:
HTTP 409 - Conflict.

Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict
in the request, such as an edit conflict.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Gone
documentation:
HTTP 410 - Gone.

Indicates that the resource requested is no longer available and will
not be available again.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.LengthRequired
documentation:
HTTP 411 - Length Required.

The request did not specify the length of its content, which is
required by the requested resource.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PreconditionFailed
documentation:
HTTP 412 - Precondition Failed.

The server does not meet one of the preconditions that the requester
put on the request.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestEntityTooLarge
documentation:
HTTP 413 - Request Entity Too Large.

The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestUriTooLong
documentation:
HTTP 414 - Request-URI Too Long.

The URI provided was too long for the server to process.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnsupportedMediaType
documentation:
HTTP 415 - Unsupported Media Type.

The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does
not support.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestedRangeNotSatisfiable
documentation:
HTTP 416 - Requested Range Not Satisfiable.

The client has asked for a portion of the file, but the server cannot
supply that portion.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ExpectationFailed
documentation:
HTTP 417 - Expectation Failed.

The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnprocessableEntity
documentation:
HTTP 422 - Unprocessable Entity.

The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic
errors.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.InternalServerError
documentation:
HTTP 500 - Internal Server Error.

A generic error message, given when no more specific message is suitable.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpNotImplemented
documentation:
HTTP 501 - Not Implemented.

The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks
the ability to fulfill the request.
NotImplemented is a python keyword.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadGateway
documentation:
HTTP 502 - Bad Gateway.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable
documentation:
HTTP 503 - Service Unavailable.

The server is currently unavailable.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.GatewayTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 504 - Gateway Timeout.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpVersionNotSupported
documentation:
HTTP 505 - HttpVersion Not Supported.

The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

>
> method of HTTP request
>

>
> URL of HTTP request
>

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.client.HTTPClient.client_request

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.strutils.int_from_bool_as_string
documentation:
Interpret a string as a boolean and return either 1 or 0.

Any string value in:

    ('True', 'true', 'On', 'on', '1')

is interpreted as a boolean True.

Useful for JSON-decoded stuff and config file parsing

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.strutils.bool_from_string
documentation:
Interpret a string as a boolean.

A case-insensitive match is performed such that strings matching 't',
'true', 'on', 'y', 'yes', or '1' are considered True and, when
`strict=False`, anything else returns the value specified by 'default'.

Useful for JSON-decoded stuff and config file parsing.

If `strict=True`, unrecognized values, including None, will raise a
ValueError which is useful when parsing values passed in from an API call.
Strings yielding False are 'f', 'false', 'off', 'n', 'no', or '0'.

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.strutils.safe_decode
documentation:
Decodes incoming text/bytes string using `incoming` if they're not

: already unicode.

```
param incoming

Text's current encoding

param errors

Errors handling policy. See here for valid
values http://docs.python.org/2/library/codecs.html

returns

text or a unicode  encoded
representation of it.

raises TypeError

If text is not an instance of str
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.strutils.safe_encode
documentation:
Encodes incoming text/bytes string using `encoding`.

If incoming is not specified, text is expected to be encoded with
current python's default encoding. (`sys.getdefaultencoding`)

```
param incoming

Text's current encoding

param encoding

Expected encoding for text (Default UTF-8)

param errors

Errors handling policy. See here for valid
values http://docs.python.org/2/library/codecs.html

returns

text or a bytestring  encoded
representation of it.

raises TypeError

If text is not an instance of str
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.strutils.string_to_bytes
documentation:
Converts a string into an float representation of bytes.

The units supported for IEC

```
Kb(it), Kib(it), Mb(it), Mib(it), Gb(it), Gib(it), Tb(it), Tib(it)
KB, KiB, MB, MiB, GB, GiB, TB, TiB
```

The units supported for SI

```
kb(it), Mb(it), Gb(it), Tb(it)
kB, MB, GB, TB
```

Note that the SI unit system does not support capital letter 'K'

```
param text

String input for bytes size conversion.

param unit_system

Unit system for byte size conversion.

param return_int

If True, returns integer representation of text
in bytes. (default: decimal)

returns

Numerical representation of text in bytes.

raises ValueError

If text has an invalid value.
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.strutils.to_slug
documentation:
Normalize string.

Convert to lowercase, remove non-word characters, and convert spaces
to hyphens.

Inspired by Django's `slugify` filter.

```
param value

Text to slugify

param incoming

Text's current encoding

param errors

Errors handling policy. See here for valid
values http://docs.python.org/2/library/codecs.html

returns

slugified unicode representation of 

raises TypeError

If text is not an instance of str
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.strutils.mask_password
documentation:
Replace password with 'secret' in message.

```
param message

The string which includes security information.

param secret

value with which to replace passwords.

returns

The unicode value of message with the password fields masked.
```

For example:

```pycon
>>> mask_password("'adminPass' : 'aaaaa'")
"'adminPass' : '***'"
>>> mask_password("'admin_pass' : 'aaaaa'")
"'admin_pass' : '***'"
>>> mask_password('"password" : "aaaaa"')
'"password" : "***"'
>>> mask_password("'original_password' : 'aaaaa'")
"'original_password' : '***'"
>>> mask_password("u'original_password' :   u'aaaaa'")
"u'original_password' :   u'***'"
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.enable_lazy
documentation:
Convenience function for configuring _() to use lazy gettext

Call this at the start of execution to enable the gettextutils._
function to use lazy gettext functionality. This is useful if
your project is importing _ directly instead of using the
gettextutils.install() way of importing the _ function.
NOTE(dhellmann): End of globals that will move to the application's
integration module.

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.install
documentation:
Install a \_() function using the given translation domain.

Given a translation domain, install a \_() function using gettext's
install() function.

The main difference from gettext.install() is that we allow
overriding the default localedir (e.g. /usr/share/locale) using
a translation-domain-specific environment variable (e.g.
NOVA_LOCALEDIR).

Note that to enable lazy translation, enable_lazy must be
called.

```
param domain

the translation domain
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.get_available_languages
documentation:
Lists the available languages for the given translation domain.

```
param domain

the domain to get languages for
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.translate
documentation:
Gets the translated unicode representation of the given object.

If the object is not translatable it is returned as-is.
If the locale is None the object is translated to the system locale.

```
param obj

the object to translate

param desired_locale

the locale to translate the message to, if None the
default system locale will be used

returns

the translated object in unicode, or the original object if
it could not be translated
```

functions.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils._translate_args
documentation:
Translates all the translatable elements of the given arguments object.

This method is used for translating the translatable values in method
arguments which include values of tuples or dictionaries.
If the object is not a tuple or a dictionary the object itself is
translated if it is translatable.

If the locale is None the object is translated to the system locale.

```
param args

the args to translate

param desired_locale

the locale to translate the args to, if None the
default system locale will be used

returns

a new args object with the translated contents of the original
```

Create a new Message object.

msgid will be used as the base unicode text. It is also possible

the msgtext parameter.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.Message.translate
documentation:
Translate this message to the desired locale.

```
param desired_locale

The desired locale to translate the message to,
if no locale is provided the message will be
translated to the system's default locale.

returns

the translated message in unicode
```

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.Message._sanitize_mod_params
documentation:
Sanitize the object being modded with this Message.

- Add support for modding 'None' so translation supports it
- Trim the modded object, which can be a large dictionary, to only
those keys that would actually be used in a translation
- Snapshot the object being modded, in case the message is
translated, it will be used as it was when the Message was created

A Message object is a unicode object that can be translated.

Translation of Message is done explicitly using the translate() method.
For all non-translation intents and purposes, a Message is simply unicode,
and can be treated as such.

classes.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.TranslationHandler

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.gettextutils.TranslationHandler.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a TranslationHandler

```
param locale

locale to use for translating messages

param target

logging.Handler object to forward
LogRecord objects to after translation
```

Handler that translates records before logging them.

The TranslationHandler takes a locale and a target logging.Handler object
to forward LogRecord objects to after translating them. This handler
depends on Message objects being logged, instead of regular strings.

The handler can be configured declaratively in the logging.conf as follows:

    [handlers]
    keys = translatedlog, translator

    [handler_translatedlog]
    class = handlers.WatchedFileHandler
    args = ('/var/log/api-localized.log',)
    formatter = context

    [handler_translator]
    class = common.log.TranslationHandler
    target = translatedlog
    args = ('zh_CN',)

If the specified locale is not available in the system, the handler will
log in the default locale.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.client.HTTPClient._extract_service_catalog
documentation:
See what the auth service told us and process the response.
We may get redirected to another site, fail or actually get
back a service catalog with a token and our endpoints.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.client.HTTPClient._fetch_endpoints_from_auth
documentation:
We have a token, but don't know the final endpoint for
the region. We have to go back to the auth service and
ask again. This request requires an admin-level token
to work. The proxy token supplied could be from a low-level enduser.

We can't get this from the keystone service endpoint, we have to use
the admin endpoint.

This will overwrite our admin token with the user token.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.build_url
documentation:
Builds a resource URL for the given kwargs.

Given an example collection where `collection_key = 'entities'` and
`key = 'entity'`, the following URL's could be generated.

By default, the URL will represent a collection of entities, e.g.:

```
/entities
```

If kwargs contains an `entity_id`, then the URL will represent a
specific member, e.g.:

```
/entities/{entity_id}
```

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

Base manager class for manipulating entities.

Children of this class are expected to define a `collection_key` and `key`.

- `collection_key`: Usually a plural noun by convention (e.g. `entities`);
  used to refer collections in both URL's (e.g.  `/v3/entities`) and JSON
  objects containing a list of member resources (e.g. `{'entities': [{},
  {}, {}]}`).
- `key`: Usually a singular noun by convention (e.g. `entity`); used to
  refer to an individual member of the collection.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.v2.client.Client.authenticate
documentation:
Authenticate against the server.

Normally this is called automatically when you first access the API,
but you can call this method to force authentication right now.

Returns on success; raises {exc}`exceptions.Unauthorized` if the
credentials are wrong.

Top-level object to access the One Cloud Storage API.

Create an instance with your creds:

```
>>> client = Client(USERNAME, PASSWORD, PROJECT_ID, AUTH_URL)
```

Then call methods on its managers:

```
>>> client.volumes.list()
...
```

autocompletion, like UUIDs or human-friendly IDs.

A resource listing will clear and repopulate the cache.

A resource create will append to the cache.

Delete is not handled because listings are assumed to be performed
often enough to keep the cache reasonably up-to-date.

methods.name: eclcli.storage.storageclient.v2.volumes.VolumeManager._format_sort_param
documentation:
Formats the sort information into the sort query string parameter.

The input sort information can be any of the following:
\- Comma-separated string in the form of \<key\[:dir\]>
\- List of strings in the form of \<key\[:dir\]>
\- List of either string keys, or tuples of (key, dir)

For example, the following import sort values are valid:
\- 'key1:dir1,key2,key3:dir3'
\- \['key1:dir1', 'key2', 'key3:dir3'\]
\- \[('key1', 'dir1'), 'key2', ('key3', dir3')\]

```
param sort

Input sort information

returns

Formatted query string parameter or None

raise ValueError

If an invalid sort direction or invalid sort key is
given
```

The input sort information can be any of the following:
\- Comma-separated string in the form of \<key\[:dir\]>
\- List of strings in the form of \<key\[:dir\]>
\- List of either string keys, or tuples of (key, dir)

For example, the following import sort values are valid:
\- 'key1:dir1,key2,key3:dir3'
\- \['key1:dir1', 'key2', 'key3:dir3'\]
\- \[('key1', 'dir1'), 'key2', ('key3', dir3')\]

```
param sort

Input sort information

returns

Formatted query string parameter or None

raise ValueError

If an invalid sort direction or invalid sort key is
given
```

documentation: Error in CLI tool.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.AuthorizationFailure

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.ConnectionRefused

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.NoUniqueMatch
documentation: Multiple entities found instead of one.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.EndpointException
documentation: Something is rotten in Service Catalog.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.EndpointNotFound
documentation: Could not find requested endpoint in Service Catalog.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.AmbiguousEndpoints
documentation: Found more than one matching endpoint in Service Catalog.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpError
documentation: The base exception class for all HTTP exceptions.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPRedirection
documentation: HTTP Redirection.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPClientError
documentation:
Client-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the client seems to have erred.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpServerError
documentation:
Server-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the server is aware that it has
erred or is incapable of performing the request.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.MultipleChoices
documentation:
HTTP 300 - Multiple Choices.

Indicates multiple options for the resource that the client may follow.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadRequest
documentation:
HTTP 400 - Bad Request.

The request cannot be fulfilled due to bad syntax.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Unauthorized
documentation:
HTTP 401 - Unauthorized.

Similar to 403 Forbidden, but specifically for use when authentication
is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.PaymentRequired
documentation:
HTTP 402 - Payment Required.

Reserved for future use.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Forbidden
documentation:
HTTP 403 - Forbidden.

The request was a valid request, but the server is refusing to respond
to it.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotFound
documentation:
HTTP 404 - Not Found.

The requested resource could not be found but may be available again
in the future.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.MethodNotAllowed
documentation:
HTTP 405 - Method Not Allowed.

A request was made of a resource using a request method not supported
by that resource.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotAcceptable
documentation:
HTTP 406 - Not Acceptable.

The requested resource is only capable of generating content not
acceptable according to the Accept headers sent in the request.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.ProxyAuthenticationRequired
documentation:
HTTP 407 - Proxy Authentication Required.

The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 408 - Request Timeout.

The server timed out waiting for the request.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Conflict
documentation:
HTTP 409 - Conflict.

Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict
in the request, such as an edit conflict.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Gone
documentation:
HTTP 410 - Gone.

Indicates that the resource requested is no longer available and will
not be available again.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.LengthRequired
documentation:
HTTP 411 - Length Required.

The request did not specify the length of its content, which is
required by the requested resource.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.PreconditionFailed
documentation:
HTTP 412 - Precondition Failed.

The server does not meet one of the preconditions that the requester
put on the request.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestEntityTooLarge
documentation:
HTTP 413 - Request Entity Too Large.

The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestUriTooLong
documentation:
HTTP 414 - Request-URI Too Long.

The URI provided was too long for the server to process.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnsupportedMediaType
documentation:
HTTP 415 - Unsupported Media Type.

The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does
not support.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestedRangeNotSatisfiable
documentation:
HTTP 416 - Requested Range Not Satisfiable.

The client has asked for a portion of the file, but the server cannot
supply that portion.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.ExpectationFailed
documentation:
HTTP 417 - Expectation Failed.

The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnprocessableEntity
documentation:
HTTP 422 - Unprocessable Entity.

The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic
errors.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.InternalServerError
documentation:
HTTP 500 - Internal Server Error.

A generic error message, given when no more specific message is suitable.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpNotImplemented
documentation:
HTTP 501 - Not Implemented.

The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks
the ability to fulfill the request.
NotImplemented is a python keyword.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadGateway
documentation:
HTTP 502 - Bad Gateway.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable
documentation:
HTTP 503 - Service Unavailable.

The server is currently unavailable.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.GatewayTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 504 - Gateway Timeout.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpVersionNotSupported
documentation:
HTTP 505 - HttpVersion Not Supported.

The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

functions.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.cliutils.validate_args
documentation:
Check that the supplied args are sufficient for calling a function.

```pycon
>>> validate_args(lambda a: None)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): a
>>> validate_args(lambda a, b, c, d: None, 0, c=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): b, d
```

```
param fn

the function to check

param arg

the positional arguments supplied

param kwargs

the keyword arguments supplied
```

classes.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager

methods.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.build_url
documentation:
Builds a resource URL for the given kwargs.

Given an example collection where `collection_key = 'entities'` and
`key = 'entity'`, the following URL's could be generated.

By default, the URL will represent a collection of entities, e.g.:

```
/entities
```

If kwargs contains an `entity_id`, then the URL will represent a
specific member, e.g.:

```
/entities/{entity_id}
```

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

documentation:

```
param base_url

methods.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.ecl.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.find
documentation:
Find a single item with attributes matching `**kwargs`.

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

Base manager class for manipulating entities.

Children of this class are expected to define a `collection_key` and `key`.

- `collection_key`: Usually a plural noun by convention (e.g. `entities`);
  used to refer collections in both URL's (e.g.  `/v3/entities`) and JSON
  objects containing a list of member resources (e.g. `{'entities': [{},
  {}, {}]}`).
- `key`: Usually a singular noun by convention (e.g. `entity`); used to
  refer to an individual member of the collection.

functions.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.client.get_client
documentation:
Get an authenticated client, based on the credentials in the kwargs.

```
param version

the API version to use ('1' or '2')

param kwargs

keyword args containing credentials, either:

session: a keystoneauth/keystoneclient session object

service_type: The default service_type for URL discovery

service_name: The default service_name for URL discovery

interface: The default interface for URL discovery

(Default: public)

region_name: The default region_name for URL discovery

endpoint_override: Always use this endpoint URL for requests

for this ceiloclient

auth: An auth plugin to use instead of the session one

user_agent: The User-Agent string to set

(Default is python-ceilometer-client)

connect_retries: the maximum number of retries that should be

attempted for connection errors

logger: A logging object

or (DEPRECATED):

os_auth_token: (DEPRECATED) pre-existing token to re-use,

use os_token instead

os_token: pre-existing token to re-use

ceilometer_url: (DEPRECATED) Ceilometer API endpoint,

use os_endpoint instead

os_endpoint: Ceilometer API endpoint

or (DEPRECATED):

os_username: name of user

os_password: user's password

os_user_id: user's id

os_user_domain_id: the domain id of the user

os_user_domain_name: the domain name of the user

os_project_id: the user project id

os_tenant_id: V2 alternative to os_project_id

os_project_name: the user project name

os_tenant_name: V2 alternative to os_project_name

os_project_domain_name: domain name for the user project

os_project_domain_id: domain id for the user project

os_auth_url: endpoint to authenticate against

os_cert|os_cacert: path of CA TLS certificate

os_key: SSL private key

os_insecure: allow insecure SSL (no cert verification)
```

functions.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.v2.options.build_url
documentation:
Convert list of dicts and a list of params to query url format.

This will convert the following:
    "[{field=this,op=le,value=34},
      {field=that,op=eq,value=foo,type=string}],
     ['foo=bar','sna=fu']"
to:
    "?q.field=this&q.field=that&
      q.op=le&q.op=eq&
      q.type=&q.type=string&
      q.value=34&q.value=foo&
      foo=bar&sna=fu"

functions.name: eclcli.monitoring.monitoringclient.v2.options.cli_to_array
documentation:
Convert CLI list of queries to the Python API format.

This will convert the following:
    "this<=34;that=string::foo"
to
    "[{field=this,op=le,value=34,type=''},
      {field=that,op=eq,value=foo,type=string}]"

classes.name: eclcli.identity.v3.mapping._RulesReader

methods.name: eclcli.identity.v3.mapping._RulesReader._read_rules
documentation:
Read and parse rules from path

Expect the file to contain a valid JSON structure.

```
param path

path to the file

return

loaded and valid dictionary with rules

raises exception.CommandError

In case the file cannot be
accessed or the content is not a valid JSON.
```

Example of the content of the file:

: \[

  : {

    : "local": \[

      : {

        : "group": {

          : "id": "85a868"

          }

        }

      \],
      "remote": \[

      > {
      >
      > : "type": "orgPersonType",
      >   "any_one_of": \[
      >
      >   > "Employee"
      >
      >   \]
      >
      > },
      > {
      >
      > > "type": "sn",
      > > "any_one_of": \[
      > >
      > > > "Young"
      > >
      > > \]
      >
      > }

      \]

    }

  \]
Helper class capable of reading rules from files

functions.name: eclcli.identity.common.find_service
documentation: Find a service by id, name or type.

functions.name: eclcli.identity.common._find_identity_resource
documentation:
Find a specific identity resource.

Using keystoneclient's manager, attempt to find a specific resource by its
name or ID. If Forbidden to find the resource (a common case if the user
does not have permission), then return the resource by creating a local
instance of keystoneclient's Resource.

The parameter identity_client_manager is a keystoneclient manager,
for example: keystoneclient.v3.users or keystoneclient.v3.projects.

The parameter resource_type is a keystoneclient resource, for example:
keystoneclient.v3.users.User or keystoneclient.v3.projects.Project.

```
param identity_client_manager

the manager that contains the resource

type identity_client_manager



param name_or_id

the resources's name or ID

type name_or_id

string

param resource_type

class that represents the resource type

type resource_type



returns

the resource in question

rtype

```

Discover the available auth-systems.

This won't take into account the old style auth-systems.

Load options needed by the available auth-systems into a parser.

This function will try to populate the parser with options from the
available plugins.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.get_auth_url
documentation: Return the auth url for the plugin (if any).

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.add_opts
documentation:
Populate and return the parser with the options for this plugin.

If the plugin does not need any options, it should return the same
parser untouched.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.parse_opts
documentation:
Parse the actual auth-system options if any.

This method is expected to populate the attribute self.opts with a
dict containing the options and values needed to make authentication.
If the dict is empty, the client should assume that it needs the same
options as the 'keystone' auth system (i.e. os_username and
os_password).

Returns the self.opts dict.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.authenticate
documentation: Authenticate using plugin defined method.

Base class for authentication plugins.

An authentication plugin needs to override at least the authenticate
method to be a valid plugin.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.auth_plugin.DeprecatedAuthPlugin
documentation:
Class to mimic the AuthPlugin class for deprecated auth systems.

Old auth systems only define two entry points: openstack.client.auth_url
and openstack.client.authenticate. This class will load those entry points
into a class similar to a valid AuthPlugin.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.service_catalog.ServiceCatalog

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.service_catalog.ServiceCatalog.url_for
documentation:
Fetch the public URL from the Compute service for
a particular endpoint attribute. If none given, return
the first. See tests for sample service catalog.
Helper methods for dealing with a Keystone Service Catalog.

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.utils.find_resource
documentation:
Look for resource in a given manager.

Used as a helper for the \_find\_\* methods.
Example:

```python
def _find_hypervisor(cs, hypervisor):
    #Get a hypervisor by name or ID.
    return cliutils.find_resource(cs.hypervisors, hypervisor)
```

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.utils.add_resource_manager_extra_kwargs_hook
documentation:
Add hook to bind CLI arguments to ResourceManager calls.

The `do_foo` calls in shell.py will receive CLI args and then in turn pass
them through to the ResourceManager. Before passing through the args, the
hooks registered here will be called, giving us a chance to add extra
kwargs (taken from the command-line) to what's passed to the
ResourceManager.

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.utils.get_resource_manager_extra_kwargs
documentation: Return extra_kwargs by calling resource manager kwargs hooks.

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.utils.pretty_choice_dict
documentation: Returns a formatted dict as 'key=value'.

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.utils._flatten
documentation:
Flatten a dict, using name as a prefix for the keys of dict.

```pycon
>>> _flatten('cpu_info', {'arch':'x86_64'})
[('cpu_info_arch': 'x86_64')]
```

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.utils.flatten_dict
documentation:
Return a new dict whose sub-dicts have been merged into the
original.  Each of the parents keys are prepended to the child's
to prevent collisions.  Any string elements will be JSON parsed
before flattening.

```pycon
>>> flatten_dict({'service': {'host':'cloud9@compute-068', 'id': 143}})
{'service_host': colud9@compute-068', 'service_id': 143}
```

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPClientError
documentation:
Client-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the client seems to have erred.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpServerError
documentation:
Server-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the server is aware that it has
erred or is incapable of performing the request.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.MultipleChoices
documentation:
HTTP 300 - Multiple Choices.

Indicates multiple options for the resource that the client may follow.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadRequest
documentation:
HTTP 400 - Bad Request.

The request cannot be fulfilled due to bad syntax.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Unauthorized
documentation:
HTTP 401 - Unauthorized.

Similar to 403 Forbidden, but specifically for use when authentication
is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PaymentRequired
documentation:
HTTP 402 - Payment Required.

Reserved for future use.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Forbidden
documentation:
HTTP 403 - Forbidden.

The request was a valid request, but the server is refusing to respond
to it.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotFound
documentation:
HTTP 404 - Not Found.

The requested resource could not be found but may be available again
in the future.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.MethodNotAllowed
documentation:
HTTP 405 - Method Not Allowed.

A request was made of a resource using a request method not supported
by that resource.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotAcceptable
documentation:
HTTP 406 - Not Acceptable.

The requested resource is only capable of generating content not
acceptable according to the Accept headers sent in the request.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ProxyAuthenticationRequired
documentation:
HTTP 407 - Proxy Authentication Required.

The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 408 - Request Timeout.

The server timed out waiting for the request.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Conflict
documentation:
HTTP 409 - Conflict.

Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict
in the request, such as an edit conflict.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Gone
documentation:
HTTP 410 - Gone.

Indicates that the resource requested is no longer available and will
not be available again.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.LengthRequired
documentation:
HTTP 411 - Length Required.

The request did not specify the length of its content, which is
required by the requested resource.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PreconditionFailed
documentation:
HTTP 412 - Precondition Failed.

The server does not meet one of the preconditions that the requester
put on the request.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestEntityTooLarge
documentation:
HTTP 413 - Request Entity Too Large.

The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestUriTooLong
documentation:
HTTP 414 - Request-URI Too Long.

The URI provided was too long for the server to process.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnsupportedMediaType
documentation:
HTTP 415 - Unsupported Media Type.

The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does
not support.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestedRangeNotSatisfiable
documentation:
HTTP 416 - Requested Range Not Satisfiable.

The client has asked for a portion of the file, but the server cannot
supply that portion.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ExpectationFailed
documentation:
HTTP 417 - Expectation Failed.

The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnprocessableEntity
documentation:
HTTP 422 - Unprocessable Entity.

The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic
errors.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.InternalServerError
documentation:
HTTP 500 - Internal Server Error.

A generic error message, given when no more specific message is suitable.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpNotImplemented
documentation:
HTTP 501 - Not Implemented.

The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks
the ability to fulfill the request.
NotImplemented is a python keyword.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadGateway
documentation:
HTTP 502 - Bad Gateway.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable
documentation:
HTTP 503 - Service Unavailable.

The server is currently unavailable.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.GatewayTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 504 - Gateway Timeout.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpVersionNotSupported
documentation:
HTTP 505 - HttpVersion Not Supported.

The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.client.HTTPClient.request
documentation:
Send an http request with the specified characteristics.

Wrapper around `requests.Session.request` to handle tasks such as
setting headers, JSON encoding/decoding, and error handling.

```
param method

method of HTTP request

param url

URL of HTTP request

param kwargs

any other parameter that can be passed to
requests.Session.request (such as ) or 
that will be encoded as JSON and used as  argument
```

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.client.HTTPClient.concat_url
documentation:
Concatenate endpoint and final URL.

E.g., "<http://keystone/v2.0/>" and "/tokens" are concatenated to
"<http://keystone/v2.0/tokens>".

```
param endpoint

the base URL

param url

the final URL
```

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.client.HTTPClient.client_request
documentation:
Send an http request using `client`'s endpoint and specified `url`.

If request was rejected as unauthorized (possibly because the token is
expired), issue one authorization attempt and send the request once
again.

```
param client

instance of BaseClient descendant

param method

method of HTTP request

param url

URL of HTTP request

param kwargs

any other parameter that can be passed to
```

modules.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.fake_client
documentation:
A fake server that "responds" to API methods with pre-canned responses.

All of these responses come from the spec, so if for some reason the spec's
wrong the tests might raise AssertionError. I've indicated in comments the
places where actual behavior differs from the spec.

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.cliutils.validate_args
documentation:
Check that the supplied args are sufficient for calling a function.

```pycon
>>> validate_args(lambda a: None)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): a
>>> validate_args(lambda a, b, c, d: None, 0, c=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): b, d
```

```
param fn

the function to check

param args

the positional arguments supplied

param kwargs

the keyword arguments supplied
```

classes.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.build_url
documentation:
Builds a resource URL for the given kwargs.

Given an example collection where `collection_key = 'entities'` and
`key = 'entity'`, the following URL's could be generated.

By default, the URL will represent a collection of entities, e.g.:

```
/entities
```

If kwargs contains an `entity_id`, then the URL will represent a
specific member, e.g.:

```
/entities/{entity_id}
```

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager._filter_kwargs
documentation: Drop null values and handle ids.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.list
documentation:
List the collection.

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.put
documentation:
Update an element.

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.find
documentation:
Find a single item with attributes matching `**kwargs`.

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

Base manager class for manipulating entities.

Children of this class are expected to define a `collection_key` and `key`.

- `collection_key`: Usually a plural noun by convention (e.g. `entities`);
  used to refer collections in both URL's (e.g.  `/v3/entities`) and JSON
  objects containing a list of member resources (e.g. `{'entities': [{},
  {}, {}]}`).
- `key`: Usually a singular noun by convention (e.g. `entity`); used to
  refer to an individual member of the collection.

functions.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.client._original_only
documentation:
Indicates and enforces that this function can only be used if we are
using the original HTTPClient object.

We use this to specify that if you use the newer Session HTTP client then
you are aware that the way you use your client has been updated and certain
functions are no longer allowed to be used.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.client.HTTPClient._redact
documentation:
Replace the value of a key in `target`.

The key can be at the top level by specifying a list with a single
key as the path. Nested dictionaries are also supported by passing a
list of keys to be navigated to find the one that should be replaced.
In this case the last one is the one that will be replaced.

:param dict target: the dictionary that may have a key to be redacted;
                    modified in place
:param list path: a list representing the nested structure in `target`
                  that should be redacted; modified in place
:param string text: optional text to use as a replacement for the
                    redacted key. if text is not specified, the
                    default text will be sha1 hash of the value being
                    redacted

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.client.HTTPClient._extract_service_catalog

See what the auth service told us and process the response.
We may get redirected to another site, fail or actually get
back a service catalog with a token and our endpoints.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.client.HTTPClient._fetch_endpoints_from_auth
documentation:
We have a token, but don't know the final endpoint for
the region. We have to go back to the auth service and
ask again. This request requires an admin-level token
to work. The proxy token supplied could be from a low-level enduser.

We can't get this from the keystone service endpoint, we have to use
the admin endpoint.

This will overwrite our admin token with the user token.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.base.Manager.completion_cache
documentation:
The completion cache store items that can be used for bash
autocompletion, like UUIDs or human-friendly IDs.

A resource listing will clear and repopulate the cache.

A resource create will append to the cache.

Delete is not handled because listings are assumed to be performed
often enough to keep the cache reasonably up-to-date.

Managers interact with a particular type of API (servers, flavors, images,
etc.) and provide CRUD operations for them.

methods.name: eclcli.dh.dhclient.v2.client.Client.authenticate
documentation:
Authenticate against the server.

Normally this is called automatically when you first access the API,
but you can call this method to force authentication right now.

Returns on success; raises {exc}`exceptions.Unauthorized` if the
credentials are wrong.

Top-level object to access the OpenStack Compute API.

Create an instance with your creds:

```
>>> client = Client(USERNAME, PASSWORD, PROJECT_ID, AUTH_URL)
```

Or, alternatively, you can create a client instance using the
keystoneclient.session API:

```
>>> from keystoneclient.auth.identity import v2
>>> from keystoneclient import session
>>> from dhclient.client import Client
>>> auth = v2.Password(auth_url=AUTH_URL,
                       username=USERNAME,
                       password=PASSWORD,
                       tenant_name=PROJECT_ID)
>>> sess = session.Session(auth=auth)
>>> dh = client.Client(VERSION, session=sess)
```

Then call methods on its managers:

It is also possible to use an instance as a context manager in which
case there will be a session kept alive for the duration of the with
statement:

It is also possible to have a permanent (process-long) connection pool,
by passing a connection_pool=True:

methods.name: eclcli.api.auth_plugin.TokenEndpoint.__init__
documentation:
A plugin for static authentication with an existing token

:param string url: Service endpoint
:param string token: Existing token

Auth plugin to handle traditional token/endpoint usage

Implements the methods required to handle token authentication
with a user-specified token and service endpoint; no Identity calls
are made for re-scoping, service catalog lookups or the like.

The purpose of this plugin is to get rid of the special-case paths
in the code to handle this authentication format. Its primary use
is for bootstrapping the Keystone database.

classes.name: eclcli.api.auth_plugin.OSCGenericPassword

methods.name: eclcli.api.auth_plugin.OSCGenericPassword.create_plugin
documentation:
Handle default Keystone endpoint configuration

Build the actual API endpoint from the scheme, host and port of the
original auth URL and the rest from the returned version URL.

Auth plugin hack to work around broken Keystone configurations

The default Keystone configuration uses http://localhost:xxxx in
admin_endpoint and public_endpoint and are returned in the links.href
attribute by the version routes.  Deployments that do not set these
are unusable with newer keystoneclient version discovery.

functions.name: eclcli.api.utils.simple_filter
documentation:
Filter a list of dicts

```
param list data

The list to be filtered.  The list is modified in-place and will
be changed if any filtering occurs.

param string attr

The name of the attribute to filter.  If attr does not exist no
match will succeed and no rows will be returned.  If attr is
None no filtering will be performed and all rows will be returned.

param sring value

The value to filter.  None is considered to be a 'no filter' value.
'' matches against a Python empty string.

param string property_field

The name of the data field containing a property dict to filter.
If property_field is None, attr is a field name. If property_field
is not None, attr is a property key name inside the named property
field.

returns

Returns the filtered list

rtype list

```

This simple filter (one attribute, one exact-match value) searches a
list of dicts to select items.  It first searches the item dict for a
matching `attr` then does an exact-match on the `value`.  If
`property_field` is given, it will look inside that field (if it
exists and is a dict) for a matching `value`.

methods.name: eclcli.api.image_v1.APIv1.image_list
documentation:
Get available images

```
param detailed

Retrieve detailed response from server if True

param public

Return public images if True

param private

Return private images if True
```

If public and private are both True or both False then all images are
returned.  Both arguments False is equivalent to no filter and all
images are returned.  Both arguments True is a filter that includes
both public and private images which is the same set as all images.

<http://docs.openstack.org/api/openstack-image-service/1.1/content/requesting-a-list-of-public-vm-images.html>
<http://docs.openstack.org/api/openstack-image-service/1.1/content/requesting-detailed-metadata-on-public-vm-images.html>
<http://docs.openstack.org/api/openstack-image-service/1.1/content/filtering-images-returned-via-get-images-and-get-imagesdetail.html>

can add limit/marker

```
param detailed

For v1 compatibility only, ignored as v2 is always 'detailed'

param public

Return public images if True

param private

Return private images if True

param shared

Return shared images if True
```

If public, private and shared are all True or all False then all
images are returned.  All arguments False is equivalent to no filter
and all images are returned.  All arguments True is a filter that
includes all public, private and shared images which is the same set
as all images.

<http://docs.openstack.org/api/openstack-image-service/2.0/content/list-images.html>

methods.name: eclcli.api.api.BaseAPI.find_attr
documentation:
Find a resource via attribute or ID

Most APIs return a list wrapped by a dict with the resource
name as key.  Some APIs (Identity) return a dict when a query
string is present and there is one return value.  Take steps to
unwrap these bodies and return a single dict without any resource
wrappers.

:param string path:
    The API-specific portion of the URL path
:param string value:
    value to search for
:param string attr:
    attribute to use for resource search
:param string resource:
    plural of the object resource name; defaults to path

For example:
    n = find(netclient, 'network', 'networks', 'matrix')
Layered actions built on top of the basic action methods do not
explicitly take a Session but one may still be passed in kwargs

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.AuthPluginOptionsMissing
documentation: Auth plugin misses some options.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.AuthSystemNotFound
documentation: User has specified an AuthSystem that is not installed.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.NoUniqueMatch
documentation: Multiple entities found instead of one.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.EndpointException
documentation: Something is rotten in Service Catalog.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.EndpointNotFound
documentation: Could not find requested endpoint in Service Catalog.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.AmbiguousEndpoints
documentation: Found more than one matching endpoint in Service Catalog.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpError
documentation: The base exception class for all HTTP exceptions.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPRedirection
documentation: HTTP Redirection.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPClientError
documentation:
Client-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the client seems to have erred.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpServerError
documentation:
Server-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the server is aware that it has
erred or is incapable of performing the request.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.MultipleChoices
documentation:
HTTP 300 - Multiple Choices.

Indicates multiple options for the resource that the client may follow.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadRequest
documentation:
HTTP 400 - Bad Request.

The request cannot be fulfilled due to bad syntax.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Unauthorized
documentation:
HTTP 401 - Unauthorized.

Similar to 403 Forbidden, but specifically for use when authentication
is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.PaymentRequired
documentation:
HTTP 402 - Payment Required.

Reserved for future use.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Forbidden
documentation:
HTTP 403 - Forbidden.

The request was a valid request, but the server is refusing to respond
to it.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotFound
documentation:
HTTP 404 - Not Found.

The requested resource could not be found but may be available again
in the future.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.MethodNotAllowed
documentation:
HTTP 405 - Method Not Allowed.

A request was made of a resource using a request method not supported
by that resource.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotAcceptable
documentation:
HTTP 406 - Not Acceptable.

The requested resource is only capable of generating content not
acceptable according to the Accept headers sent in the request.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.ProxyAuthenticationRequired
documentation:
HTTP 407 - Proxy Authentication Required.

The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 408 - Request Timeout.

The server timed out waiting for the request.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Conflict
documentation:
HTTP 409 - Conflict.

Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict
in the request, such as an edit conflict.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.Gone
documentation:
HTTP 410 - Gone.

Indicates that the resource requested is no longer available and will
not be available again.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.LengthRequired
documentation:
HTTP 411 - Length Required.

The request did not specify the length of its content, which is
required by the requested resource.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.PreconditionFailed
documentation:
HTTP 412 - Precondition Failed.

The server does not meet one of the preconditions that the requester
put on the request.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestEntityTooLarge
documentation:
HTTP 413 - Request Entity Too Large.

The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestUriTooLong
documentation:
HTTP 414 - Request-URI Too Long.

The URI provided was too long for the server to process.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnsupportedMediaType
documentation:
HTTP 415 - Unsupported Media Type.

The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does
not support.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestedRangeNotSatisfiable
documentation:
HTTP 416 - Requested Range Not Satisfiable.

The client has asked for a portion of the file, but the server cannot
supply that portion.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.ExpectationFailed
documentation:
HTTP 417 - Expectation Failed.

The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnprocessableEntity
documentation:
HTTP 422 - Unprocessable Entity.

The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic
errors.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.InternalServerError
documentation:
HTTP 500 - Internal Server Error.

A generic error message, given when no more specific message is suitable.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpNotImplemented
documentation:
HTTP 501 - Not Implemented.

The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks
the ability to fulfill the request.
NotImplemented is a python keyword.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadGateway
documentation:
HTTP 502 - Bad Gateway.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable
documentation:
HTTP 503 - Service Unavailable.

The server is currently unavailable.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.GatewayTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 504 - Gateway Timeout.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpVersionNotSupported
documentation:
HTTP 505 - HttpVersion Not Supported.

The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

alphabetical order.

argparse.Namespace

documentation:
Return token and endpoint.

```
param service_type

Service type of the endpoint

type service_type

string

param endpoint_type

Type of endpoint.
Possible values: public or publicURL,
internal or internalURL,
admin or adminURL

string

returns

tuple of token and endpoint strings

raises

EndpointException
```

Base class for authentication plugins.

An authentication plugin needs to override at least the authenticate
method to be a valid plugin.

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.client.HTTPClient.request
documentation:
Send an http request with the specified characteristics.

Wrapper around `requests.Session.request` to handle tasks such as
setting headers, JSON encoding/decoding, and error handling.

```
param method

method of HTTP request

param url

URL of HTTP request

param kwargs

any other parameter that can be passed to
requests.Session.request (such as ) or 
that will be encoded as JSON and used as  argument
```

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.client.HTTPClient.concat_url
documentation:
Concatenate endpoint and final URL.

E.g., "<http://keystone/v2.0/>" and "/tokens" are concatenated to
"<http://keystone/v2.0/tokens>".

```
param endpoint

the base URL

param url

the final URL
```

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.client.HTTPClient.client_request
documentation:
Send an http request using `client`'s endpoint and specified `url`.

If request was rejected as unauthorized (possibly because the token is
expired), issue one authorization attempt and send the request once
again.

```
param client

instance of BaseClient descendant

param method

method of HTTP request

param url

URL of HTTP request

param kwargs

any other parameter that can be passed to
```

class_variables.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.client.BaseClient.endpoint_type

Top-level object to access the OpenStack API.

This client uses {class}`HTTPClient` to send requests. {class}`HTTPClient`
will handle a bunch of issues such as authentication.

    u'raid_card_hardware_id': u'd27d25d2-0a47-4c0d-bb48-888c7b8545a5',

\]

in fact, it's a dictionary;

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.get_auth_url
documentation: Return the auth url for the plugin (if any).

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.add_opts
documentation:
Populate and return the parser with the options for this plugin.

If the plugin does not need any options, it should return the same
parser untouched.

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.parse_opts
documentation:
Parse the actual auth-system options if any.

This method is expected to populate the attribute self.opts with a
dict containing the options and values needed to make authentication.
If the dict is empty, the client should assume that it needs the same
options as the 'keystone' auth system (i.e. os_username and
os_password).

Returns the self.opts dict.

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.authenticate
documentation: Authenticate using plugin defined method.

Base class for authentication plugins.

An authentication plugin needs to override at least the authenticate
method to be a valid plugin.

classes.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.auth_plugin.DeprecatedAuthPlugin
documentation:
Class to mimic the AuthPlugin class for deprecated auth systems.

Old auth systems only define two entry points: openstack.client.auth_url
and openstack.client.authenticate. This class will load those entry points
into a class similar to a valid AuthPlugin.

functions.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.cliutils.validate_args
documentation:
Check that the supplied args are sufficient for calling a function.

```pycon
>>> validate_args(lambda a: None)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): a
>>> validate_args(lambda a, b, c, d: None, 0, c=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): b, d
```

```
param fn

the function to check

param arg

the positional arguments supplied

param kwargs

the keyword arguments supplied
```

functions.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.utils.add_resource_manager_extra_kwargs_hook
documentation:
Add hook to bind CLI arguments to ResourceManager calls.

The `do_foo` calls in shell.py will receive CLI args and then in turn pass
them through to the ResourceManager. Before passing through the args, the
hooks registered here will be called, giving us a chance to add extra
kwargs (taken from the command-line) to what's passed to the
ResourceManager.

functions.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.utils.get_resource_manager_extra_kwargs
documentation: Return extra_kwargs by calling resource manager kwargs hooks.

documentation: Returns a formatted dict as 'key=value'.

functions.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.utils._flatten
documentation:
Flatten a dict, using name as a prefix for the keys of dict.

```pycon
>>> _flatten('cpu_info', {'arch':'x86_64'})
[('cpu_info_arch': 'x86_64')]
```

functions.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.utils.flatten_dict
documentation:
Return a new dict whose sub-dicts have been merged into the
original.  Each of the parents keys are prepended to the child's
to prevent collisions.  Any string elements will be JSON parsed
before flattening.

```pycon
>>> flatten_dict({'service': {'host':'cloud9@compute-068', 'id': 143}})
{'service_host': colud9@compute-068', 'service_id': 143}
```

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.ecl.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.build_url
documentation:
Builds a resource URL for the given kwargs.

Given an example collection where `collection_key = 'entities'` and
`key = 'entity'`, the following URL's could be generated.

By default, the URL will represent a collection of entities, e.g.:

```
/entities
```

If kwargs contains an `entity_id`, then the URL will represent a
specific member, e.g.:

```
/entities/{entity_id}
```

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

Base manager class for manipulating entities.

Children of this class are expected to define a `collection_key` and `key`.

- `collection_key`: Usually a plural noun by convention (e.g. `entities`);
  used to refer collections in both URL's (e.g.  `/v3/entities`) and JSON
  objects containing a list of member resources (e.g. `{'entities': [{},
  {}, {}]}`).
- `key`: Usually a singular noun by convention (e.g. `entity`); used to
  refer to an individual member of the collection.

functions are no longer allowed to be used.

documentation: def get_client_class(version):
version_map = {
'2': 'bareclient.v2.client.Client',
try:
client_path = version_map[str(version)]
except (KeyError, ValueError):
raise
return importutils.import_class(client_path)
