methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.client.HTTPClient._redact
documentation:
Replace the value of a key in `target`.

The key can be at the top level by specifying a list with a single
key as the path. Nested dictionaries are also supported by passing a
list of keys to be navigated to find the one that should be replaced.
In this case the last one is the one that will be replaced.

:param dict target: the dictionary that may have a key to be redacted;
                    modified in place
:param list path: a list representing the nested structure in `target`
                  that should be redacted; modified in place
:param string text: optional text to use as a replacement for the
                    redacted key. if text is not specified, the
                    default text will be sha1 hash of the value being
                    redacted

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.client.HTTPClient._extract_service_catalog
documentation:
See what the auth service told us and process the response.
We may get redirected to another site, fail or actually get
back a service catalog with a token and our endpoints.

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.client.HTTPClient._fetch_endpoints_from_auth
documentation:
We have a token, but don't know the final endpoint for
the region. We have to go back to the auth service and
ask again. This request requires an admin-level token
to work. The proxy token supplied could be from a low-level enduser.

We can't get this from the keystone service endpoint, we have to use
the admin endpoint.

This will overwrite our admin token with the user token.

documentation:
The completion cache store items that can be used for bash
autocompletion, like UUIDs or human-friendly IDs.

A resource listing will clear and repopulate the cache.

A resource create will append to the cache.

Delete is not handled because listings are assumed to be performed
often enough to keep the cache reasonably up-to-date.

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.add_opts
documentation:
Populate and return the parser with the options for this plugin.

If the plugin does not need any options, it should return the same

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.parse_opts
documentation:
Parse the actual auth-system options if any.

This method is expected to populate the attribute self.opts with a
dict containing the options and values needed to make authentication.
If the dict is empty, the client should assume that it needs the same
options as the 'keystone' auth system (i.e. os_username and
os_password).

Returns the self.opts dict.

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.auth_plugin.BaseAuthPlugin.authenticate
documentation: Authenticate using plugin defined method.

Base class for authentication plugins.

An authentication plugin needs to override at least the authenticate
method to be a valid plugin.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.auth_plugin.DeprecatedAuthPlugin
documentation:
Class to mimic the AuthPlugin class for deprecated auth systems.

Old auth systems only define two entry points: openstack.client.auth_url
and openstack.client.authenticate. This class will load those entry points
into a class similar to a valid AuthPlugin.

functions.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.utils.add_resource_manager_extra_kwargs_hook
documentation:
Add hook to bind CLI arguments to ResourceManager calls.

The `do_foo` calls in shell.py will receive CLI args and then in turn pass
them through to the ResourceManager. Before passing through the args, the
hooks registered here will be called, giving us a chance to add extra
kwargs (taken from the command-line) to what's passed to the
ResourceManager.

documentation: Return extra_kwargs by calling resource manager kwargs hooks.

documentation: Returns a formatted dict as 'key=value'.

functions.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.utils._flatten
documentation:
Flatten a dict, using name as a prefix for the keys of dict.

```pycon
>>> _flatten('cpu_info', {'arch':'x86_64'})
[('cpu_info_arch': 'x86_64')]
```

functions.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.utils.flatten_dict
documentation:
Return a new dict whose sub-dicts have been merged into the
original.  Each of the parents keys are prepended to the child's
to prevent collisions.  Any string elements will be JSON parsed
before flattening.

```pycon
>>> flatten_dict({'service': {'host':'cloud9@compute-068', 'id': 143}})
{'service_host': colud9@compute-068', 'service_id': 143}
```

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpError
documentation: The base exception class for all HTTP exceptions.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPRedirection
documentation: HTTP Redirection.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPClientError
documentation:
Client-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the client seems to have erred.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpServerError
documentation:
Server-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the server is aware that it has
erred or is incapable of performing the request.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.MultipleChoices
documentation:
HTTP 300 - Multiple Choices.

Indicates multiple options for the resource that the client may follow.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadRequest
documentation:
HTTP 400 - Bad Request.

The request cannot be fulfilled due to bad syntax.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Unauthorized
documentation:
HTTP 401 - Unauthorized.

Similar to 403 Forbidden, but specifically for use when authentication
is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PaymentRequired
documentation:
HTTP 402 - Payment Required.

Reserved for future use.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Forbidden
documentation:
HTTP 403 - Forbidden.

The request was a valid request, but the server is refusing to respond
to it.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotFound
documentation:
HTTP 404 - Not Found.

The requested resource could not be found but may be available again
in the future.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.MethodNotAllowed
documentation:
HTTP 405 - Method Not Allowed.

A request was made of a resource using a request method not supported
by that resource.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotAcceptable
documentation:
HTTP 406 - Not Acceptable.

The requested resource is only capable of generating content not
acceptable according to the Accept headers sent in the request.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ProxyAuthenticationRequired
documentation:
HTTP 407 - Proxy Authentication Required.

The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 408 - Request Timeout.

The server timed out waiting for the request.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Conflict
documentation:
HTTP 409 - Conflict.

Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict
in the request, such as an edit conflict.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Gone
documentation:
HTTP 410 - Gone.

Indicates that the resource requested is no longer available and will
not be available again.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.LengthRequired
documentation:
HTTP 411 - Length Required.

The request did not specify the length of its content, which is
required by the requested resource.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PreconditionFailed
documentation:
HTTP 412 - Precondition Failed.

The server does not meet one of the preconditions that the requester
put on the request.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestEntityTooLarge
documentation:
HTTP 413 - Request Entity Too Large.

The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestUriTooLong
documentation:
HTTP 414 - Request-URI Too Long.

The URI provided was too long for the server to process.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnsupportedMediaType
documentation:
HTTP 415 - Unsupported Media Type.

The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does
not support.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestedRangeNotSatisfiable
documentation:
HTTP 416 - Requested Range Not Satisfiable.

The client has asked for a portion of the file, but the server cannot
supply that portion.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ExpectationFailed
documentation:
HTTP 417 - Expectation Failed.

The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnprocessableEntity
documentation:
HTTP 422 - Unprocessable Entity.

The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic
errors.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.InternalServerError
documentation:
HTTP 500 - Internal Server Error.

A generic error message, given when no more specific message is suitable.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpNotImplemented
documentation:
HTTP 501 - Not Implemented.

The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks
the ability to fulfill the request.
NotImplemented is a python keyword.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadGateway
documentation:
HTTP 502 - Bad Gateway.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable
documentation:
HTTP 503 - Service Unavailable.

The server is currently unavailable.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.GatewayTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 504 - Gateway Timeout.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpVersionNotSupported
documentation:
HTTP 505 - HttpVersion Not Supported.

The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

class_variables.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.client.BaseClient.endpoint_type
documentation: "publicURL" will be used

Top-level object to access the OpenStack API.

This client uses {class}`HTTPClient` to send requests. {class}`HTTPClient`
will handle a bunch of issues such as authentication.

Base class for authentication plugins.

An authentication plugin needs to override at least the authenticate
method to be a valid plugin.

functions.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.cliutils.validate_args
documentation:
Check that the supplied args are sufficient for calling a function.

```pycon
>>> validate_args(lambda a: None)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): a
>>> validate_args(lambda a, b, c, d: None, 0, c=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): b, d
```

```
param fn

the function to check

param arg

the positional arguments supplied

param kwargs

the keyword arguments supplied
```

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.v2.servers.ServerManager.create
documentation:
Create (boot) a new server.

```
param name

Something to name the server.

param image

The  to boot with.

param flavor

The  to boot onto.

param meta

A dict of arbitrary key/value metadata to store for this
server. A maximum of five entries is allowed, and both
keys and values must be 255 characters or less.

param files

A dict of files to overrwrite on the server upon boot.
Keys are file names (i.e. /etc/passwd) and values
are the file contents (either as a string or as a
file-like object). A maximum of five entries is allowed,
and each file must be 10k or less.

param reservation_id

a UUID for the set of servers being requested.

param min_count

(optional extension) The minimum number of
servers to launch.

param max_count

(optional extension) The maximum number of
servers to launch.

param security_groups

A list of security group names

param userdata

user data to pass to be exposed by the metadata
server this can be a file type object as well or a
string.

param key_name

(optional extension) name of previously created
keypair to inject into the baremetal.

param availability_zone

Name of the availability zone for baremetal
placement.

param block_device_mapping

(optional extension) A dict of block
device mappings for this server.

param block_device_mapping_v2

(optional extension) A dict of block
device mappings for this server.

param nics

(optional extension) an ordered list of nics to be
added to this server, with information about
connected networks, fixed IPs, port etc.

param scheduler_hints

(optional extension) arbitrary key-value pairs
specified by the client to help boot an baremetal

param config_drive

(optional extension) value for config drive
either boolean, or volume-id

param disk_config

(optional extension) control how the disk is
partitioned when the server is created.  possible
values are 'AUTO' or 'MANUAL'.
```

methods.name: eclcli.bare.bareclient.v2.servers.ServerManager.rebuild
documentation:
Rebuild -- shut down and then re-image -- a server.

```
param server

The  (or its ID) to share onto.

param image

the  (or its ID) to re-image with.

param password

string to set as password on the rebuilt server.

param disk_config

partitioning mode to use on the rebuilt server.
Valid values are 'AUTO' or 'MANUAL'

param preserve_ephemeral

If True, request that any ephemeral device
be preserved when rebuilding the baremetal. Defaults to False.

param name

Something to name the server.

param meta

A dict of arbitrary key/value metadata to store for this
server. A maximum of five entries is allowed, and both
keys and values must be 255 characters or less.

param files

A dict of files to overwrite on the server upon boot.
Keys are file names (i.e. /etc/passwd) and values
are the file contents (either as a string or as a
file-like object). A maximum of five entries is allowed,
and each file must be 10k or less.
```

documentation:
Resize a server's resources.

```
param server

The  (or its ID) to share onto.

param flavor

the  (or its ID) to resize to.

param disk_config

partitioning mode to use on the rebuilt server.
Valid values are 'AUTO' or 'MANUAL'
```

Until a resize event is confirmed with {meth}`confirm_resize`, the old
server will be kept around and you'll be able to roll back to the old
flavor quickly with {meth}`revert_resize`. All resizes are
automatically confirmed after 24 hours.

def add_security_group(self, server, security_group):
Add a Security Group to an baremetal
:param server: ID of the baremetal.
:param security_group: The name of security group to add.
self._action('addSecurityGroup', server, {'name': security_group})
def remove_security_group(self, server, security_group):
Add a Security Group to an baremetal
:param server: ID of the baremetal.
:param security_group: The name of security group to remove.
self._action('removeSecurityGroup', server, {'name': security_group})
def list_security_group(self, server):
List Security Group(s) of an baremetal
:param server: ID of the baremetal.
return self._list('/servers/%s/os-security-groups' %
base.getid(server), 'security_groups',
security_groups.SecurityGroup)

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.common.apiclient.base.CrudManager.build_url
documentation:
Builds a resource URL for the given kwargs.

Given an example collection where `collection_key = 'entities'` and
`key = 'entity'`, the following URL's could be generated.

By default, the URL will represent a collection of entities, e.g.:

```
/entities
```

If kwargs contains an `entity_id`, then the URL will represent a
specific member, e.g.:

```
/entities/{entity_id}
```

```
param base_url

if provided, the generated URL will be appended to it
```

documentation:


Base manager class for manipulating entities.

Children of this class are expected to define a `collection_key` and `key`.

- `collection_key`: Usually a plural noun by convention (e.g. `entities`);
  used to refer collections in both URL's (e.g.  `/v3/entities`) and JSON
  objects containing a list of member resources (e.g. `{'entities': [{},
  {}, {}]}`).
- `key`: Usually a singular noun by convention (e.g. `entity`); used to
  refer to an individual member of the collection.

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.client.HTTPClient._redact
documentation:
Replace the value of a key in `target`.

The key can be at the top level by specifying a list with a single
key as the path. Nested dictionaries are also supported by passing a
list of keys to be navigated to find the one that should be replaced.
In this case the last one is the one that will be replaced.

:param dict target: the dictionary that may have a key to be redacted;
                    modified in place
:param list path: a list representing the nested structure in `target`
                  that should be redacted; modified in place
:param string text: optional text to use as a replacement for the
                    redacted key. if text is not specified, the
                    default text will be sha1 hash of the value being
                    redacted

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.client.HTTPClient._extract_service_catalog
documentation:
See what the auth service told us and process the response.
We may get redirected to another site, fail or actually get
back a service catalog with a token and our endpoints.

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.client.HTTPClient._fetch_endpoints_from_auth
documentation:
We have a token, but don't know the final endpoint for
the region. We have to go back to the auth service and
ask again. This request requires an admin-level token
to work. The proxy token supplied could be from a low-level enduser.

We can't get this from the keystone service endpoint, we have to use
the admin endpoint.

This will overwrite our admin token with the user token.

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.client.HTTPClient._v2_auth
documentation: Authenticate against a v2.0 auth service.

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.client.HTTPClient._authenticate
documentation: Authenticate and extract the service catalog.

methods.name: eclcli.rca.rcaclient.base.Manager.completion_cache
documentation:
The completion cache store items that can be used for bash
autocompletion, like UUIDs or human-friendly IDs.

A resource listing will clear and repopulate the cache.

A resource create will append to the cache.

Delete is not handled because listings are assumed to be performed
often enough to keep the cache reasonably up-to-date.

etc.) and provide CRUD operations for them.
